caption: An educational presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" is displayed, featuring a slide titled "Tutorial – 2". The content focuses on the "Work/unit mass of gas compressed," presenting a formula $W/m = T_1(s_1 - s_2) - (h_1 - h_2)$. A thermodynamic diagram is shown with a plotted cycle on axes of temperature (T) and entropy (s), featuring labels such as "T=const", "D=const", and "p=const". The NPTEL logo is visible in the bottom-left corner, and a footer attributes the material to "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay". As the sequence progresses, a cursor highlights a portion of the formula. In the latter half of the sequence, a data table is introduced below the text, listing values for pressure (p in bar), temperature (T in K), enthalpy (h in J/g), and entropy (s in J/gK) for states 1, 2, and f.
